by_id.reason: Asserts 201, exact echoed body, and exactly one persisted quotas row queried directly from Postgres.
Asserts 200 on replace, updated limit, and still exactly one row (verified via direct SQL read, not just the app's own echo).
Ingest-scoped key gets 403 code=forbidden.
Both missing and malformed Bearer tokens get 401.
0/-1/-1000/non-integer limit, unknown field, and bad customer_id all assert 422 validation_failed at both the unit-schema and endpoint layers.

The endpoint test proves the app-level api_key_id scoping keeps two tenants' rows independent. Independently added this session: test_quotas_rls_backstop.py proves the DB-level RLS BACKSTOP itself (not just the app filter) -- it connects as a provisioned NOBYPASSRLS role (mirroring the production meterly_app role, not the testcontainers superuser, which always bypasses RLS) and issues queries with the api_key_id predicate REMOVED ENTIRELY (the primary control disabled), asserting the RLS policy alone still confines reads/updates to the caller's own tenant, and that an unset tenant setting fails closed (zero rows) rather than defaulting open. This satisfies the adversarial-test-shapes requirement that a backstop control must be proven with the primary control off, not merely declared.
No quota row -> 201 for a very large quantity, no regression.
R+Q<=L -> 201, rollup total verified via direct SQL read.
429 quota_exceeded + Retry-After >= 1s; direct SQL proves the rejected event left no row and the rollup only reflects the accepted event (transaction rolled back).
First event in an empty window with Q>L -> 429; direct SQL proves zero rollup rows created.
A replay after the quota is lowered below the accumulated total still returns 200 with idempotent_replay=true and event_count stays 1 (quota never consulted on replay).
Lowering L below the already-accumulated R blocks the very next POST (429) -- fresh-read-per-request proven.

20 concurrent distinct-idempotency-key posts against L=10 land exactly 10 winners/10 rejections (429 quota_exceeded), and the final rollup total via direct SQL never exceeds L -- asserts the loser's outcome, not merely a single-winner count.

Tier-2 throttle fires first (code=rate_limited) via the full request lifecycle (not a unit test of the key generator); a genuinely quota-exceeded call on a separate untouched-throttle key returns the distinct quota_exceeded code.
Two admin principals sharing one client IP (10.0.0.9) get independent Tier-2 buckets -- the discriminating two-principals-one-IP shape, not a same-owner-different-IP weak form.

Seeds 2 api_keys rows + a quotas row, runs up->down(0002)->up, asserts: quotas table (create-migration) is fully absent after down (schema-only reversibility, no row-survival claim); the 2 pre-existing api_keys rows survive with scope resetting to the 'ingest' default (expand/contract add-column contract); and after re-up, every constraint (PK duplicate, FK to nonexistent api_key, CHECK limit>=1 for 0 and -5, and api_keys.scope CHECK) is independently re-driven and rejected -- not merely asserted present in a schema dump.
--admin sets scope='admin'; omitting it defaults to 'ingest'.

SQLi/path-traversal/CRLF/oversized payloads in customer_id and metric are rejected at the Pydantic boundary (422) before reaching quotas_repo's parameterized text() sinks; the Hypothesis property test additionally proves any customer_id that DOES parse matches the anchored allowlist over generated inputs, not fixed examples.
quota.rejected (WARNING, no current_total/limit fields), quota.upsert, and quota.forbidden are each asserted present in structlog JSON stdout.

Human-revised AC20 (2026-07-09 debugging escalation, see .pipeline/debug-notes.md and the AC20 row in acceptance.md): now a RELATIVE budget (quota-active p95 <= 1.5x the same-session no-quota baseline p95, equal uvicorn worker budget) rather than the unachievable-on-this-host absolute 50ms figure. Renamed from test_events_ingest_with_quotas_p95_under_50ms, which previously only asserted sample_count>0 (a test-quality gap flagged in test-quality.json) -- the rewritten test now drives BOTH the no-quota baseline and the quota-active load in the same test/session at equal worker budget (5 workers each, asserted equal via _quota_perf_workers()==_baseline_perf_workers()) and directly asserts quota_p95<=1.5*baseline_p95. Measured this run: baseline p95=124.10ms (7467 samples, 497.98 rps), quota-active p95=123.45ms (7441 samples, 496.95 rps) -- quota path is essentially AT PARITY with baseline (ratio 0.995x), comfortably inside the 1.5x bound. This also resolves the earlier finding: the prior 3362ms measurement was a harness artifact (2-worker quota fixture vs 5-worker baseline), root-caused and fixed by debugging; with equal workers the quota FOR UPDATE read-and-decide path adds no material latency.

The served /openapi.json includes PUT /v1/quotas, and the DAST auth-context doc test confirms the Bearer mtr_live_<key_id>_<secret> scheme is documented; docs/system_architecture.md documents the admin test-key requirement (seed_api_key.py --admin).
ASVS reconciliation is the security stage's deliverable, not a test-suite assertion.

budget.note: AC20 human-revised at the 2026-07-09 debugging escalation to a RELATIVE bound (quota-active p95 <= 1.5x same-session no-quota baseline p95, equal worker budget); the absolute 50ms figure moved to the post-merge CI/staging load-campaign budget, out of this stage's scope.

scenario.harness: grafana/k6 via Docker, out-of-process uvicorn reached via host.docker.internal, constant-arrival-rate scenario
scenario.duration: 5s warmup + 15s measurement, run for both the no-quota baseline and the quota-active load in the same test/session
scenario.workload: POST /v1/events against 50 pre-seeded customer_id buckets; quota-active run has a high-but-finite quota per bucket (limit never exceeded) so the FOR UPDATE quota read-and-decide runs on every request without ever rejecting; baseline run has no quota rows at all
scenario.contention: quota row lock (FOR UPDATE) taken per request across 50 distinct (api_key_id, customer_id, metric) rows, not a single hot lock
scenario.worker_budget: 5 uvicorn workers for both the baseline and quota-active runs (equal, asserted by the test itself before measuring)
scenario.note: Root-caused via debugging (.pipeline/debug-notes.md): the prior 3362ms/40x-gap measurement was a harness artifact -- the quota perf fixture ran only 2 uvicorn workers vs the baseline's 5, so excess arrivals queued under the 500rps constant-arrival scenario. Fixed to equal worker budget (5/5); quota-active p95 is now essentially at parity with baseline (ratio 0.995x), well inside the human-revised 1.5x bound.
